title: Buttery Polish Whipping Cream Dumplings
description: A lazy-sunday polish dish featuring long-fermentation-dough, gluten-free-friendly and creamy whipping cream. Best served as a dessert.

ingredients.name: gluten-free flour
ingredients.notes: use a combination of rice flour, almond flour and potato starch

ingredients.name: whole milk
ingredients.notes: use cold milk for best results

instructions: Combine gluten-free flour, sugar and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Add melted butter, seltzer water and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rest in a warm place for 2 hours or until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and roll it out to a thickness of 1 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or a glass.
Place a spoonful of whipped cream in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Place the dumplings on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the dumplings with beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Bake the dumplings in a preheated oven at 37570f for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Dust the dumplings with powdered sugar and serve warm.
